rulings.Case Title: DANIEL BOREEN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notcie Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 29, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Request For Judicial Notice. Granted without leave to amend. The Second Amended Petition is barred in its entirety by the 90-day statute of limitations provided in Code of Civil Procedure 1094.6(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LATHEM BONEM VS. MV TRAANSPORTATION, INC. , S. SEYOUME et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 29, 2012, Line 17, DEFENDANT MV TRANSPORTATION, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied because defendant failed to maintain its initial burden. Defendant fails to show that it was an intended third party beneficiary of the Bonem/Shefman settlement. Defendant fails to show that res judicata or collateral estoppel applies. The two actions did not involve the same primary right, same parties or privies, same causes of action and the same issues were not adjudicated. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: TERRIE WILLIAMS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 29, 2012, Line 16, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurer to the second cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff cannot plead that claim against a public entity such as defendant as a matter of law. See Miklosy v. Regents of University of California, 44 Cal.4th 876; Anthoine v. N. Cent. Counties Consortium (9th Cir. 2010) 605 F.3d 740. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
